title: Taraxacum ustkanense

The Taraxacum genus, specifically the common dandelion (Taraxacum officinale), is a perennial herbaceous plant characterized by its bright yellow flowers and jagged, toothed leaves. The plant is known for its numerous health benefits, including its ability to support liver and kidney function, aid in digestion, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Taraxacum officinale include taraxasterol, taraxol, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Taraxacum officinale include tea, capsules, and tinctures, which are often used to support liver and kidney health, as well as to reduce inflammation and improve digestion.
